title: 📚 API Documentation
subtitle: Public v1 API — reserved to platform subscribers. Create keys from the « API keys » page in your tenant's admin portal.

reference.loadFailed: Load failed — check your session or retry.

intro.title: 1. What is the public API for?
intro.p1: The v1 public API lets an external system (CRM, form, orchestrator, BI) drive TaskMgr like an operator: create, update, transition work orders, manage clients and addresses, upload attachments.
intro.p2: It's distinct from the internal frontend API: dedicated /api/v1/* path, API-key auth (no JWT), tenant carried by the key (no subdomain), more generous rate limits.

auth.title: 2. Create an API key
auth.p1: Only a tenant ADMIN can create keys — from the « 🔑 API keys » page in the sidebar:
auth.step2: Pick a scope: read-only, read-write, or admin (see below).
auth.warning: ⚠️ The plaintext (tkm_…) is shown ONLY ONCE at creation. Copy it to your vault immediately.

scopes.title: 4. Permission model (scopes)
scopes.p1: Hierarchy: admin ⊇ read-write ⊇ read-only. Every endpoint documents its minimum scope.
scopes.readOnly: GET only. Perfect for a BI dashboard or external reporting.
scopes.readWrite: Create, update, delete on business resources. Recommended for most integrations.
scopes.admin: Includes tenant settings changes. Reserve for internal orchestrators.

examples.title: 5. End-to-end examples
examples.transitionExplain: The status field on PATCH is FORBIDDEN in the public API — transitions go through a dedicated endpoint validated by the process engine.

rateLimit.title: 6. Rate limiting
rateLimit.p1: Three named buckets in parallel. Every response includes X-RateLimit-* headers per bucket:
rateLimit.defaults: Defaults per key: 30 req/s · 300 req/10s · 3000 req/min. A 429 includes a Retry-After header.

security.title: 7. Security
security.storage: Only the SHA-256 hash is stored — the plaintext is never recoverable after creation.
security.revoke: Revocation is instant — the next request receives 401.
security.tenant: The tenant is carried by the key. No way for a tenant A's key to access tenant B's data.
security.audit: All creations, revocations, and calls are audited and cross-tenant readable by the SA.

notIncluded.title: 8. Not exposed in v1
notIncluded.p1: Some resources are intentionally kept out of the public perimeter:
notIncluded.users: Users — create/update/delete (privilege escalation risk). Only GET /technicians is exposed.
notIncluded.templates: Work order templates — CRUD reserved (internal system config).
notIncluded.processes: Processes — CRUD reserved (sensitive state machine). GET /processes/:id/snapshot read-only is available.
notIncluded.webhooks: Webhooks (outbound events) — deferred to v2. Poll the endpoints in the meantime.
